title: Secure script community partner + co-sign orthography & design brief
context: M2 is explicitly blocked on a community partner — no glyph drawing for any under-served script may begin until a named script authority or language community has been secured and has co-signed the orthography and design brief. This is the M2 entry gate, as defined in PLAN.md. Depends on the script gap analysis (fonts-research-004) having produced a prioritized shortlist and the governance protocol (fonts-gov-006) being in place.
objective: Secure a named script authority or language-community partner, complete community co-design outreach, and co-produce and co-sign an orthography specification and design brief for the target script before any glyph drawing begins.
acceptanceCriteria: A named community or script-authority partner confirms the need for the font in writing; verifiedNeed is flipped to true on this task and all dependent M2 tasks once this confirmation is received.
The partner is credited as co-designer in all project artifacts (FONTLOG, provenance.json, README); right to be named or anonymous respected.
Orthography specification, target language(s), glyph repertoire, and OpenType shaping requirements are co-signed by the community partner.
Community veto and change-request process is acknowledged by both parties per the governance protocol (fonts-gov-006).
Reserved Font Name is agreed with the partner before proceeding; any trademark conflict identified and resolved.
Document is CC-BY-4.0; no identifiable personal data included without explicit consent.
resources: fonts-research-004 (gap analysis and prioritization shortlist — must be completed first)
output: A co-signed orthography specification and design brief document (CC-BY-4.0) for the target script, with recorded community partner confirmation, co-design protocol acknowledgement, and Reserved Font Name agreement, merged into the repository.
